element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary attack tool throughout all stages
Defensive utility for blocking certain projectiles
First special gas type, learned through stage exploration
Alternative special gas with different properties
Choose from 3 stages; unlock second set of 3 after completion
Defeat The Liquidator, Megavolt, Dr. Bushroot, Quackerjack, Professor Moliarty, Wolfduck
Final fortress stage leading to Steelbeak confrontation

typical_relief_triggers: Switching to preferred special gas weapon
typical_tension_triggers: Running out of ammunition for special weapons
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Mega Man-style stage-based structure with alternating platforming and combat intensity; player-directed stage selection allows tension modulation

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Special gas weapon pickups optional—player can complete stages with standard Gas Gun only

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory stage selection (3 initial stages of variable difficulty); Gas Gun mastery and basic platforming
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Second set of 3 stages unlocked after initial completion; increased enemy density and platform complexity; special weapon management becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Steelbeak final fortress after all 6 bosses defeated; most challenging encounters and refined enemy patterns
notable_spikes: Individual boss encounters at end of each stage (requires weapon strategy and pattern learning)
Steelbeak final confrontation (condensed Mega Man-style endgame)

notable_level_patterns.description: 6 themed stages each ending in a unique boss fight; non-linear progression allows player to choose stage order and difficulty ramp; mirrors Mega Man 5 architecture
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Fearsome Five stages (Liquidator, Megavolt, Bushroot, Quackerjack) + original characters (Professor Moliarty, Wolfduck)

notable_level_patterns.description: Special gas weapons (Thunder, Heavy, Arrow) are single-inventory items that replace each other; player must choose strategic loadout per stage or mid-combat, adding resource management tension
Projectile deflection via cape button (Up on D-pad) protects against specific enemy attacks; creates defense-focused moments distinct from pure platforming
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Throughout all stages with projectile-heavy enemies
